CSS.title: Introduction to CSS
CSS.content: CSS (Cascading Style Sheets) is used to style and layout web pages. It controls colors, fonts, spacing, and positioning.

CSS.title: CSS Syntax
CSS.content: CSS consists of selectors and declarations. A declaration includes a property and a value. Example: color: red;

CSS.title: Selectors
CSS.content: Selectors are used to target HTML elements. Examples include element selectors, class selectors, and ID selectors.

CSS.title: Colors and Backgrounds
CSS.content: CSS allows you to change text colors, background colors, and add gradients or images.

CSS.title: Box Model
CSS.content: Every element is a box consisting of margin, border, padding, and content. Understanding the box model is essential.

CSS.title: Flexbox
CSS.content: Flexbox is a layout system that helps align and distribute space among items in a container.

CSS.title: Grid
CSS.content: CSS Grid is a powerful layout system for creating complex designs.

CSS.title: Responsive Design
CSS.content: Responsive design ensures your website looks good on all devices using media queries.
